disclaimer: End-of-day snapshot compiled from public sources for informational purposes only — not validated for trading or investment decisions. Yield curve from Fed H.15 Apr 30 official close (May 1 publication pending; used Apr 30 as prior, Apr 29 as prior-prior).
marketPulse: Nasdaq Tops 25,000 First Time. Apple +3%, Software AI Confirmed. Iran Peace Proposal Drops Oil. Powell's Final Day Begins.
marketPulseSubtext: S&P 500 +0.29% to 7,230.12 (new record), Nasdaq +0.89% to 25,114.44 (first close above 25,000, new record). Dow -0.31% to 49,499.27 — only laggard as value/cyclicals rotated out. Russell 2000 +0.46% to 2,812.82. Apple +3.24% powered mega-cap tech after Q2 blowout. Atlassian +29.6%, Twilio +23.8%, Five9 +30% confirmed enterprise software AI monetisation. Roblox -17% on guidance slash. Iran sent updated peace proposal through Pakistan mediators — Brent fell nearly 2% to $108.17, WTI -3% to $101.94. Trump said 'not satisfied' with the offer but signal significant. War Powers Resolution 60-day deadline active. UAE OPEC+ exit effective today. Exxon and Chevron profits collapsed despite record oil prices.
topDriver: Apple +3.24% on Q2 blowout + enterprise software AI monetisation confirmed (Atlassian +30%, Twilio +24%, Five9 +30%) overcomes Iran noise. Nasdaq crosses 25,000 for first time.
narrativeSummary: May 1 was a session defined by two competing stories settling into a coherent signal. On one side: Apple's +3.24% gap confirmed the iPhone and Services franchise is intact, and Atlassian, Twilio, and Five9 each surging 20-30% on AI-powered enterprise revenue beats answered the question Meta and Microsoft raised on Wednesday — AI is absolutely monetising in the enterprise software layer. The contrast is now crisp: infrastructure AI (GOOGL, CAT) and enterprise software AI (Atlassian, Twilio, Five9, Apple) are working; social AI (META) and productivity-suite AI (MSFT) are still in the prove-it phase. On the other side: Iran's updated peace proposal to Pakistan mediators sent Brent crude nearly 2% lower to $108.17 and WTI down 3% to $101.94, the clearest de-escalation signal in weeks. But Trump's 'not satisfied' response kept the structural supply shock intact. The UAE OPEC+ exit — effective today — adds a new variable to oil market dynamics, with the UAE now free to ramp production independently. Exxon and Chevron reported Q1 earnings that collapsed 45% and 36% respectively despite record oil prices, a stark signal that the Hormuz disruption is cutting into the very companies positioned to benefit. ISM Manufacturing came in at 48.7, below the 50 expansion threshold — a recession-adjacent data point that the market absorbed without panic. The Fed transition story deepened: Powell's April 29 meeting was his last as Chair, Kevin Warsh advancing through Senate Banking. The Dow's -0.31% underperformance reflects the rotation away from industrials and value after Thursday's big CAT-driven Dow run.
macroStressSubtext: Iran peace proposal drops oil; equity records reduce stress. Hormuz physically closed, War Powers deadline, Powell exit keep regime elevated.
rateCurveRead: Yields eased modestly on May 1 as Iran's peace proposal to Pakistan mediators provided marginal relief on the long end. The 10Y pulled back 4bps to 4.38% and the 30Y eased 3bps to 4.95% — still historically elevated but off the post-PCE highs. The 2Y edged down 2bps to 3.90% as markets digested the ISM Manufacturing miss at 48.7 (contraction territory) alongside the Fed's on-hold stance. The 2/10 spread held at +48bps, still the widest since 2022. The curve is telling a consistent story: the economy is slowing slightly (ISM below 50), inflation is sticky (PCE 3.5%), and the Fed under Warsh is an unknown variable. Note: Apr 30 H.15 used as current (May 1 pending 4:15pm publication). Source: Federal Reserve H.15.

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.title: War Powers deadline triggers political crisis; Iran deal collapses
scenarios.outcome: Congress refuses to authorise Iran military action under War Powers Act. Trump forced to withdraw or faces constitutional crisis. Market uncertainty spikes. Warsh Senate vote delayed. Oil market dislocates on policy vacuum. S&P loses 5-8% in a week.
scenarios.trigger: Congress invokes War Powers Resolution AND Warsh Senate confirmation stalls past June

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.title: Iran peace talks slowly progress, AI bifurcation trade extends, Warsh confirmed
scenarios.outcome: Iran deal takes 4-6 weeks to finalise. Oil holds $100-115 range. Enterprise software AI monetisation drives Nasdaq higher. Warsh confirmed by Senate in May. Powell stays as Governor. S&P consolidates 7,200-7,500 range.
scenarios.trigger: Incremental Iran deal progress AND Warsh confirmation vote by end of May

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.title: Iran deal signed, Hormuz reopens, Warsh signals rate cut path
scenarios.outcome: Iran accepts US terms, Hormuz reopens within 2-3 weeks. Oil collapses to $80s. Energy inflation resolves rapidly. Warsh signals data-dependent cut path. S&P to 7,800+, the broadest rally of 2026.
scenarios.trigger: Hormuz reopens within 3 weeks AND Warsh first statement signals cut optionality

flows.asset: Enterprise Software AI — TEAM, TWLO, FIVN, SaaS ETFs
flows.note: Atlassian +30%, Twilio +24%, Five9 +30% on AI enterprise revenue beats triggered rotation into enterprise SaaS from capex-heavy social AI. This is the demand-side confirmation the market needed after Meta's capex shock.

Apple +3.24% on Q2 blowout and Q3 guide 14-17% drove broad mega-cap tech inflows. Nasdaq crossed 25,000 for first time. QQQ posted largest single-day inflow in 3 weeks as the consumer device AI thesis was validated.

Iran peace proposal dropped oil 2-3%. Exxon and Chevron both reported profit declines despite record prices — Iran war is disrupting operations, not just prices. Structural vulnerability exposed. XLE down on the day.
ISM Manufacturing 48.7 (contraction) reinforced Fed on-hold and recession-adjacent read. 5.2% T-bill yield continues to attract cash. Warsh transition uncertainty adding to short-end parking demand.
PCE 3.5% still the dominant inflation signal. TIPS positioning maintained even with oil pulling back — market not convinced Iran deal closes the energy inflation chapter yet.
Profit-taking after CAT's +10% session Thursday. Dow -0.31% underperformance reflects industrial rotation. Value and cyclicals lagged on the day as the session was tech-led.
Colgate-Palmolive beat Q1 estimates and raised guidance, demonstrating pricing power. Defensive rotation into staples mild but notable given ISM manufacturing miss signalling slowing growth.
Cboe Global Markets +9% on strong earnings — volatility and options volumes surging on Iran and earnings uncertainty. Financials led by fee-based businesses benefiting from elevated market activity.

flowsRead: May 1 flows confirm the AI bifurcation trade is widening and deepening. The session's two dominant inflows — Apple/mega-cap tech ($2.8B) and enterprise SaaS ($2.1B) — both reflect AI monetisation confirmation, but from different angles: Apple from the consumer device ecosystem, Atlassian/Twilio/Five9 from enterprise subscription revenue. Energy outflows ($1.8B) accelerated as the Exxon/Chevron profit collapse revealed that the Iran war is operationally destructive to energy majors, not just a pricing windfall. The rotation is clean: money leaving industrials and energy, entering AI beneficiaries and short-duration safety. TIPS inflows persisting even as oil falls suggests institutional investors see inflation as multi-source, not purely energy-driven.

topThemes.theme: Enterprise software AI monetisation confirmed — Atlassian +30%, Twilio +24%, Five9 +30%, Apple +3%
topThemes.impact: AI demand-side fully confirmed. Enterprise customers paying for AI products at scale. Atlassian cloud rev +29%, Twilio rev +20%, Five9 subscription rev +13% — all accelerating. Apple iPhone +22%, Services record $31B. AICS aiRevCapexRatio now 0.86, approaching parity.

topThemes.theme: Iran peace proposal — updated terms sent via Pakistan mediators, Trump 'not satisfied', War Powers deadline active
topThemes.impact: Brent -1.9% to $108.17, WTI -3% to $101.94 on peace signal. Trump's rejection kept structural supply shock intact. 60-day War Powers deadline creates legal uncertainty. Hormuz remains at 4% normal flow.

topThemes.theme: Energy major profit collapse — Exxon -45% NI, Chevron -36% NI despite record oil prices
topThemes.impact: Iran war is operationally destructive to energy companies, not a pricing windfall. Disrupted production, stuck shipments, surging costs. New OGPI signal: high price + falling major profits = supply chain damage deeper than expected.

topThemes.theme: ISM Manufacturing 48.7 (contraction) + Fed transition — Powell last day, Warsh advancing, 9.1% hike priced
topThemes.impact: Manufacturing in contraction territory for first time since Feb. Combined with PCE 3.5%, GDP 2.0% — growth is cooling, inflation is not. Fed transition to Warsh adds unknown policy path variable. CME hike probability rose to 9.1%.

topThemes.theme: UAE OPEC+ exit effective today — 59 years of membership ends, cartel cohesion fractures
topThemes.impact: UAE free to produce independently, eroding OPEC's coordinating power. ING: 'big blow' to OPEC, welcomed by Trump. Near-term impact limited by Hormuz closure, but structural change to oil market governance is significant.

crossMarket.driver: Second consecutive record close. S&P and Nasdaq both at all-time highs. Tech-led, value lagged. Apple and enterprise SaaS the engines. ISM Manufacturing 48.7 absorbed without panic.
Nasdaq crossed 25,000 for the first time ever. Apple +3.24%, Atlassian +29.6%, Twilio +23.8%, Five9 +30% drove the index. Enterprise software AI monetisation the dominant catalyst.
Dow only major index to fall. Profit-taking in industrials after CAT's +10% Thursday run. Value and cyclicals rotated out as session was purely tech-driven. Briefly tested 50,000 before reversing.
Small caps continued higher, approaching 52-week high. Breadth story intact. Russell up 11.4% for the week ending May 1. Wolfspeed +26% on executive appointments added small-cap energy.
VIX nudged up slightly on Iran news complexity — peace proposal sent but Trump rejected it. Net: geopolitical uncertainty slightly higher, but equity record kept fear low. Sub-17 regime maintained.

Gapped up on Apr 30 AH Q2 beat. iPhone +22% March record, Services $31B record, Q3 guide 14-17%. Cook confirmed memory crunch ongoing but demand exceeds supply — positive constraint framing. Cook last public appearance before Ternus takes over Sep 1.
Q3 rev $1.79B +32% YoY, cloud rev +29%, RPO +37%. AI-powered enterprise products driving acceleration. Best day in years. Counter-narrative to Meta/MSFT: enterprise AI is monetising at scale.
Q1 rev $1.41B +20% YoY, dollar-based NER 114%, guided FY rev growth 14-15% up from 11.5-12.5%. CEO: 'foundational infrastructure layer in the era of AI.' Free cash flow $132M.
Q1 beat on EPS and revenue, subscription rev +13% accelerating 2nd consecutive quarter. Enterprise AI agent products driving larger, longer commitments. Raised FY guidance.

Slashed full-year 2026 bookings guidance — child safety protocols (age verification, content monitoring) causing 'continued short-term friction,' slowing user acquisition. 35% of US users unable to access social features. Platform AI safety friction has real revenue consequences.

Q1 net income -45% despite record oil prices. Iran war disrupting operations and shipments, not providing windfall. Exxon beat estimates but on depressed bar. Revenue missed as Hormuz disruption constrained production.

Q1 NI -36% to $2.21B. Adjusted EPS $1.41 beat $0.95 estimate but revenue missed. CEO flagged Middle East risks. Net debt-to-cash flow worsening. Same structural story as XOM: record prices not translating to profits.
Strong Q1 earnings — options and derivatives volumes surging on Iran uncertainty and earnings season activity. Financials led by fee-based models benefiting from elevated market activity.

Iran sent updated peace proposal to Pakistan mediators. Pakistani officials confirmed delivery to US. Trump 'not satisfied' but signal is significant — clearest diplomatic movement in weeks. Structural supply shock intact as Hormuz remains at 4% normal flow.

WTI fell through $103 on Iran proposal. War Powers Resolution 60-day deadline adds Trump withdrawal risk. US crude exports at record 6mb/d as buyers seek alternatives. IEA demand destruction signal active.

Modest relief on Iran peace signal and ISM Manufacturing 48.7 (contraction read). Still elevated from PCE 3.5% print. 2/10 spread +48bps. Fed transition to Warsh adds rate path uncertainty — 9.1% hike prob vs 0% a week ago.

Gold eased slightly as Iran peace signal reduced acute safe-haven demand. Still elevated on stagflation and supply shock thesis. Monthly gain strong. Real rates at 1.92% (10Y TIPS) competing with non-yield thesis.
Dollar hit 2-month low on Iran peace optimism and risk-on tone. DXY weakening reflects broad risk appetite improvement and Iran de-escalation signal. Down ~2.3% for April, extending May 1.

sectorHeat.driver: Apple +3%, Atlassian +30%, Twilio +24%, Five9 +30%. Nasdaq 25,000 first ever close.
AI software beats vs Roblox -17% on safety friction. Net positive but divergence widening.
XOM -45% NI, CVX -36% NI despite record oil. Operational destruction vs pricing windfall.
Cboe +9% on record derivatives volumes. Fee-based models benefiting from Iran/earnings uncertainty.
Colgate-Palmolive beat and raised guidance. Defensive inflows on ISM contraction signal.
Dow -0.31% underperformance. CAT profit-taking after +10% Thursday. ISM Manufacturing 48.7.
Estee Lauder +12% on Q3 beat. Moderna and other healthcare names stable. Defensive rotation mild.
Enterprise software AI beats validate full stack: cloud (GOOGL), physical (CAT), device (AAPL), SaaS (TEAM/TWLO/FIVN).

drivers.name: Enterprise Software AI Monetisation — Atlassian, Twilio, Five9 all beat on AI-driven revenue
drivers.detail: Three enterprise software companies reported AI-driven acceleration on the same night: Atlassian cloud rev +29%, RPO +37%; Twilio rev +20%, NER 114%, FY guide raised to 14-15%; Five9 subscription rev +13% accelerating. All three attributed growth explicitly to enterprise AI adoption. This is the demand-side proof that was missing from the capex-heavy Meta/MSFT narrative. AICS aiRevCapexRatio now 0.86, approaching the 1.0 parity threshold where infrastructure spend is fully backed by revenue.

drivers.name: Iran Peace Proposal — Updated terms sent via Pakistan, Trump 'not satisfied', War Powers clock active
drivers.linked: Brent, WTI, XLE, OGPI, War Powers
drivers.detail: Pakistan officials confirmed Iran sent an updated peace proposal to US mediators — the clearest diplomatic movement since the ceasefire. Brent fell 1.9% and WTI fell 3% on the news. Trump's 'not satisfied' response kept the structural supply shock intact, but his comment that 'no one knows the status of talks aside from myself and a handful of others' suggests negotiations are more advanced than appearances suggest. The War Powers Resolution 60-day deadline adds a legal deadline pressure on the administration. Trump admin claiming ceasefire 'terminated' hostilities — a contested legal position.

drivers.name: Energy Major Profit Collapse — Exxon -45% NI, Chevron -36% NI despite record oil prices
drivers.detail: New OGPI signal: record oil prices are not translating to profits for energy majors because the Iran war is operationally destructive — disrupted production, stuck shipments through Hormuz, surging logistics costs. Exxon NI fell 45% despite oil at $110+. Chevron NI fell 36%. Both beat on depressed analyst estimates. This reveals the Hormuz closure has deeper supply chain consequences than just the price signal. US crude exports surged to record 6mb/d as buyers seek alternatives, but that revenue is partially offset by soaring operational costs.

drivers.name: Fed Transition + ISM Manufacturing 48.7 — Powell's last day, Warsh advancing, hike probability rising
drivers.detail: ISM Manufacturing came in at 48.7 for April — below the 50 expansion threshold for the first time since February. Combined with PCE 3.5%, GDP 2.0%, and jobless claims at 50-year lows, the stagflation profile is sharpening: goods sector now contracting while services remain hot. The Fed transition adds amplifying uncertainty — Warsh's policy preferences are hawkish-leaning, and CME FedWatch showed hike probability rising from 0% to 9.1% post-FOMC. Three dissenters wanted to remove the easing bias. New indicator input fedTransitionRisk: 74/100.

drivers.name: UAE OPEC+ Exit — Effective Today, 59 Years of Membership Ends
drivers.detail: UAE exited OPEC effective May 1 without prior consultation with Saudi Arabia, ending 59 years of membership. ING called it 'a big blow to OPEC' and noted it would be 'welcomed by Trump as it erodes OPEC's influence.' UAE Energy Minister described it as a 'strategic policy choice aligned with national interests.' New indicator input opecCohesionScore: 62/100 (down from implied 85+ pre-exit). Near-term impact limited by Hormuz closure, but structural: UAE free to ramp production independently, reducing OPEC's ability to manage supply in a crisis.

risks.title: War Powers Resolution deadline — Trump faces mandatory troop withdrawal, legal gray zone claim
risks.desc: The 60-day War Powers clock on Iran military action expired around April 29. Trump admin claiming the ceasefire 'terminated' hostilities, removing the legal trigger. Congress has not authorised the military action. If Congress formally invokes the resolution and demands withdrawal, it creates a constitutional confrontation that markets are not pricing. A forced withdrawal order could cause an oil spike above $130 as blockade collapses and Iran controls Hormuz again.

risks.title: Fed transition policy shock — Warsh replaces Powell May 15, hawkish tilt, 9.1% hike probability
risks.desc: Kevin Warsh advances through Senate Banking. Powell's term ends May 15. Three FOMC dissenters wanted to remove easing bias at the April 29 meeting — the most dissent since 1992. CME FedWatch shows hike probability at 9.1% for December, vs 0% a week ago. Warsh has historically favored tighter policy. If he inherits a PCE print above 3.5% at his first meeting, a hike in 2026 moves from fringe to mainstream. New indicator fedTransitionRisk: 74.

risks.title: ISM Manufacturing in contraction — goods sector slowdown while inflation stays elevated
risks.desc: ISM Manufacturing 48.7 for April — below 50 for the first time since February. Manufacturing is contracting even as services inflation runs hot. This is the stagflation dynamic in its clearest form: goods demand cooling (lowering GDP growth) while services and energy inflation stay elevated (preventing rate cuts). If ISM drops below 45, recession probability pricing in credit markets would accelerate sharply.

risks.title: Energy major operational destruction — Hormuz damage deeper than oil price signal suggests
risks.desc: Exxon -45% NI, Chevron -36% NI despite record oil is a warning: the Hormuz closure isn't just a price shock, it's operationally destroying the energy supply chain. Logistics costs, stuck shipments, lost production access are hitting profits even as prices soar. IEA warned countries could face acute oil shortages as final Gulf shipments have already arrived at destinations. The longer the blockade holds, the more severe the structural damage to global energy infrastructure becomes.

risks.title: Platform AI safety friction — Roblox -17% shows product changes create real revenue damage
risks.desc: Roblox slashed FY guidance, citing child safety protocols (age verification, content monitoring) causing 'continued short-term friction.' 35% of US users unable to access social features. This is the first major example of AI safety / regulatory compliance translating directly into a massive revenue miss. As AI platforms face more regulatory pressure — EU AI Act, US child safety rules — this risk broadens to other consumer-facing AI companies.

timeline.headline: Apple +3% gap open on Q2 blowout; Atlassian, Twilio, Five9 all surge 20-30% after hours
timeline.effect: Enterprise software AI monetisation confirmed overnight. Nasdaq futures pointed sharply higher. Set the dominant narrative for the session.

timeline.headline: Market opens: Nasdaq leads, Dow lags — Apple and software AI vs industrial profit-taking
timeline.effect: Immediate rotation visible: tech higher, industrials lower. Dow tested 50,000 briefly in early session before reversing on CAT profit-taking.

timeline.headline: S&P Global PMI Manufacturing final (April): 50.2, slightly expansionary
timeline.effect: Minor relief vs ISM later in the day. Services PMI remained strong. Manufacturing on the edge.

timeline.headline: ISM Manufacturing April: 48.7 — below 50, contraction territory
timeline.effect: Goods sector now contracting. Combined with PCE 3.5% = clearest stagflation read yet. 10Y eased slightly. Dow extended losses.

timeline.headline: Exxon and Chevron Q1 results: profits collapse 45% and 36% despite record oil prices
timeline.effect: Energy sector sold off. Revealed Hormuz closure is operationally destructive to majors, not a windfall. New dimension to OGPI energy major profit impact.

timeline.headline: UAE OPEC+ exit effective — 59-year membership ends, production no longer coordinated
timeline.effect: Absorbed with limited immediate oil price reaction. ING noted 'big blow to OPEC.' Structural change to oil market governance begins today.

timeline.headline: Trump tells reporters: 'not satisfied' with Iran peace proposal, will maintain blockade
timeline.effect: Oil gave back some of its morning decline. Brent stabilised around $108-109. Trump's separate comment suggesting he knows more than he's saying kept de-escalation hope alive.

timeline.headline: CNBC confirms Iran sent updated peace proposal via Pakistan mediators, delivered to US
timeline.effect: Brent dropped to session lows near $108. WTI fell to $102. War Powers deadline pressure visible as market read this as diplomatic progress regardless of Trump's public stance.

timeline.headline: Nasdaq closes above 25,000 for first time; S&P 7,230.12; Russell 2,812.82
timeline.effect: Second consecutive record close for S&P and Nasdaq. Week closes with both indexes at all-time highs. Apple +3.24% the single largest index contributor. Dow -0.31% sole laggard.

watchpoints.event: FOMC minutes release — detail on 3 dissenters, Warsh transition, hike bias debate
CoreWeave Q1 earnings — AI cloud infrastructure demand signal (Deutsche Bank Buy $125 PT)
Iran-US: War Powers Resolution legal challenge — Congress response to admin's 'terminated hostilities' claim
CPI April — if >3.5%, hike probability from 9% accelerates; Fed Warsh inaugural meeting framing
Powell officially steps down as Fed Chair — Warsh era begins
Warsh first public statement as Chair — rate path signal, easing bias question
Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting (first without Buffett as CEO — Greg Abel)
WWDC — Apple AI strategy under incoming CEO Ternus; iOS 27 Siri AI reveal

strategies.title: Long enterprise software AI — TEAM, TWLO, FIVN, IGV
strategies.body: Atlassian, Twilio, and Five9 delivered the demand-side proof that was missing after Meta/MSFT's capex shock. Enterprise customers are signing larger, longer AI contracts. NER above 100% (Twilio 114%) means existing customers are expanding. AICS enterpriseSoftwareAIRevGrowth at 20.3% — strong and accelerating. This trade has more room as the market rotates from AI capex fear to AI revenue confirmation.
strategies.trigger: Enterprise SaaS cohort revenue growth >15% for 2nd consecutive quarter

strategies.title: Short duration / Long TIPS — stagflation + Fed transition hedge
strategies.body: ISM Manufacturing 48.7 confirms goods deflation alongside PCE 3.5% services/energy inflation — stagflation in its purest form. Warsh transition adds a hike risk premium that was zero a week ago (9.1% hike probability now). 30Y at 4.95% still historically elevated. fedTransitionRisk at 74/100. Short TLT, long TIP, long BIL is the cleanest expression of this regime.
strategies.trigger: May CPI >3.3% AND Warsh first statement removes easing bias

strategies.title: Long AAPL — cook to Ternus transition de-risked, supply constraint is demand-positive
strategies.body: Apple +3.24% gap confirms the market agrees: best March quarter ever, Q3 guide 14-17%, Services record $31B, supply constraints framed as demand exceeding supply. Memory crunch warning from Cook is a risk but Ternus is an operator's operator — product execution story likely strengthens not weakens. $100B buyback is a floor. 34x P/E supportable at 17% revenue growth.
strategies.trigger: Q3 revenue guide maintained at 14-17% AND Services growth >12% in next quarter

strategies.title: Iran war options hedge — Brent put spreads for deal scenario, Brent calls for re-escalation
strategies.body: Iran sent an updated peace proposal today — the most concrete diplomatic movement since ceasefire. WTI at $101.94 is the lowest level since the blockade intensified. If a deal closes, Brent could fall to $85-90 quickly. If War Powers forces a US withdrawal, Brent could spike to $130+. A strangle structure (both puts and calls) captures either outcome. opecCohesionScore drop to 62 with UAE exit adds supply upside uncertainty to the put-side logic.
strategies.trigger: Hormuz reopening confirmed OR War Powers Congressional resolution filed

strategies.title: Monitor fedTransitionRisk — position for Warsh hike risk if CPI >3.5%
strategies.body: New indicator fedTransitionRisk at 74. If May CPI prints above 3.5% at Warsh's first meeting (June), the 9.1% hike probability could jump to 25-30%. Short 2Y Treasuries and long USD would be the expression. This is a tail risk that the market has started pricing but not fully discounting. Warsh's public statements pre-confirmation have leaned hawkish on inflation.
strategies.trigger: May CPI >3.5% AND Warsh public statement signals neutral-to-hike bias within 30 days of confirmation
